2605.12718.title: CHAL: Council of Hierarchical Agentic Language
2605.12718.paper_summary: Multi-agent debate has emerged as a promising approach for improving LLM reasoning on ground-truth tasks, yet current methodologies face certain structural limitations: debate tends to induce a martingale over belief trajectories, majority voting accounts for most observed gains, and LLMs exhibit confidence escalation rather than calibration across rounds. We argue that the genuine value of debate, and dialectic systems as a whole, lies not in ground-truth tasks but in defeasible domains, where every position can in principle be defeated by better reasoning. We present the Council of Hierarchical Agentic Language (CHAL), a multi-agent dialectic framework that treats defeasible argumentation as an engine for belief optimization. Each agent maintains a CHAL Belief Schema (CBS), a graph-structured belief representation with a Bayesian-inspired architecture, that facilitates belief revision through a gradient-informed dynamic mechanism by leveraging the strength of the belief's thesis as a differentiable objective. Meta-cognitive value systems spanning epistemology, logic, and ethics are elevated to configurable hyperparameters governing agent reasoning and adjudication outcomes. We provide a series of ablation experiments that demonstrate systematic and interpretable effects: the adjudicator's value system determines the debate's overall trajectories in latent belief space, council diversity refines beliefs for all participants, and the framework generalizes across broad fields. CHAL is, to our knowledge, the first framework to treat multi-agent debate as structured belief optimization over defeasible domains. Further, the auditable belief artifacts it produces establish the foundation for dedicated evaluation suites for defeasible argumentation, with broader implications for building AI systems whose reasoning and value commitments are transparent, aligned, and subject to human oversight.
2605.12718.paper_summary_zh: 多智能體辯論已成為改善大型語言模型（LLM）在真實任務上推理的有希望的方法，然而目前的方法面臨某些結構性限制：辯論往往會在信念軌跡上引發馬丁蓋爾，主要投票占據了大部分觀察到的增益，而LLM在回合之間表現出信心升高而非校準。我們認為辯論的真正價值，以及整體辯證系統，不在於真實任務，而在於可推翻的領域，在這裡每個立場原則上都可以被更好的推理所擊敗。我們提出了層次性代理語言委員會（CHAL），這是一個多智能體辯證框架，將可推翻的論證視為信念優化的引擎。每個代理維護一個CHAL信念架構（CBS），這是一個圖結構的信念表示，具有啟發於貝葉斯的架構，通過利用信念論題的強度作為可微分的目標，促進信念修訂，並通過梯度信息的動態機制進行。跨越認識論、邏輯和倫理的元認知價值系統被提升為可配置的超參數，這些參數控制代理的推理和裁決結果。我們提供了一系列消融實驗，展示了系統性和可解釋的效果：裁決者的價值系統決定了辯論在潛在信念空間中的整體軌跡，委員會多樣性為所有參與者精煉信念，而該框架在廣泛領域中具有普遍性。據我們所知，CHAL是第一個將多智能體辯論視為可推翻領域上的結構化信念優化的框架。此外，它所產生的可審計信念文物為專門的可推翻論證評估套件奠定了基礎，並對構建推理和價值承諾透明、對齊且受人類監督的AI系統具有更廣泛的影響。
2605.12718.author: Tommaso Giovannelli et.al.
